pattern: Sorting both sides and walking them with two pointers greedily pairs each player to the smallest trainer that can still serve them, maximizing matches.
hints: Try to satisfy the weakest player first using the smallest trainer that is strong enough.
If both lists are sorted, a single sweep can decide every pairing without backtracking.
Advance a player pointer only when the current trainer's capacity covers it; always advance the trainer pointer, counting a match on success.

edge_inputs.description: two of three players can be matched
one trainer for many identical players
single player too strong for single trainer
exact equality counts as a match
more trainers than players, all match

stress.description: random abilities and capacities of differing lengths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    m = max(1, rng.randrange(1, size + 1))
    players = [rng.randrange(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    trainers = [rng.randrange(1, 1000) for _ in range(m)]
    return [players, trainers]
stress.note: varied lengths and overlapping ranges stress the greedy pairing
